title: The Agency AI Agent ROI Calculator: Payback Period and 3-Year Net on a $50K Build
subtitle: For agency owners and ops directors at 10–200 person firms. Plug in your build quote and workflow hours, get payback in months and 3-year net return in 30 seconds.

inputs.label: AI Agent Build Cost
inputs.hint: Fixed-price quote from your build partner. Domain-trained agents run $40K–$120K; agentic with retrieval $60K–$200K.

inputs.label: Annual Maintenance & API Cost
inputs.hint: Hosting, model API spend, monitoring, retainer. Default to 15–25% of build cost if unknown.

inputs.label: Hours per Week on the Workflow
inputs.hint: Total team hours, not one person. Add up everyone who touches the workflow.

inputs.label: Loaded Hourly Cost of the Team
inputs.hint: Salary + benefits + overhead. Account managers and ops $65–$120; senior strategists $150–$250.

inputs.label: Billable Rate for That Hour
inputs.hint: What you charge clients, or the rate freed hours get redeployed at. Set to 0 if hours stay internal.

inputs.label: Realistic Redeployment Rate
inputs.hint: Percent of saved hours that actually become billable work. Most firms land at 50–70%; 100% is fantasy.

inputs.label: Expected Error Rate Reduction
inputs.hint: Share of rework and missed deliverables the system removes. Default to 8% if unsure.

inputs.label: Build Timeline Until Live
inputs.hint: The window where you're paying without saving anything yet. Most builds ship in 2–4 months.

recommendations.text: The workflow isn't bleeding enough hours to justify a custom build. Either volume is too low or loaded cost is too low — look elsewhere or revisit when you've grown into the spend.
Math works but margins are thin. Cut the build cost by tightening scope to your 2–3 highest-volume deliverables, not all five. Re-run the calculator with the lower quote.
Solid ROI. Build the full workflow but enforce the 90-Day Payback Rule on phase 1 — if phase 1 doesn't clear in 90 days, don't fund phase 2.
The workflow is hemorrhaging value. Consider expanding to adjacent processes (reporting, renewals, intake) in the same build to compress fixed costs across more recovered hours.
Payback is over 9 months. The 3-year math still works, but you're carrying the cost a long time. Consider phasing the build or negotiating milestone-based payment to shorten cash exposure.

You're assuming over 75% of saved hours convert to billable work. Sales pipeline doesn't expand just because capacity does. Re-run at 60% — if the math still clears, you're safe; if it collapses, you've found the soft assumption.
Your maintenance budget is under 15% of build cost. Agentic workflows with retrieval can hit $500–$2,000/month in API alone at agency volume. Pad the number before you sign.

capture_cta.headline: Want me to pressure-test your numbers?
capture_cta.description: Send me your inputs — build quote, hours/week, loaded cost, billable rate — and I'll run the calculator with you and flag the assumptions that look soft before you sign the SOW.
